vulnerability.title: Record Unsuccessful Access Attempts to Files - open_by_handle_at
vulnerability.description: At a minimum, the audit system should collect unauthorized file accesses for all users and root. If the auditd daemon is configured to use the augenrules program to read audit rules during daemon startup (the default), add the following lines to a file with suffix .rules in the directory /etc/audit/rules.d: -a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access -a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access If the system is 64 bit then also add the following lines: -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access If the auditd daemon is configured to use the auditctl utility to read audit rules during daemon startup, add the following lines to /etc/audit/audit.rules file: -a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access -a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access If the system is 64 bit then also add the following lines: -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q audit && rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # First perform the remediation of the syscall rule # Retrieve hardware architecture of the underlying system [ "$(getconf LONG_BIT)" = "32" ] && RULE_ARCHS=("b32") || RULE_ARCHS=("b32" "b64") AUID_FILTERS="-F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset" SYSCALL="open_by_handle_at" KEY="access" SYSCALL_GROUPING="creat ftruncate truncate open openat open_by_handle_at" for ARCH in "${RULE_ARCHS[@]}" do ACTION_ARCH_FILTERS="-a always,exit -F arch=$ARCH" OTHER_FILTERS="-F exit=-EACCES" # Perform the remediation for both possible tools: 'auditctl' and 'augenrules' unset syscall_a unset syscall_grouping unset syscall_string unset syscall unset file_to_edit unset rule_to_edit unset rule_syscalls_to_edi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves adding audit rules for the open_by_handle_at syscall, a standard STIG hardening action that does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystem integrity. It is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential audit log growth if many failed attempts occur; Minor performance overhead from additional audit checks

reasoning_messages.reasoning: We need to follow workflow: Provide plan, call review_plan. Developer message: we must submit plan via review_plan. So first message includes plan. After approval, apply. So produce plan first.

review_feedback: The proposed plan omits the required leading hyphen on the '-a' rule keyword and does not account for whether the system uses auditctl rather than augenrules, potentially rendering the added rules ineffective. Additionally, it does not include truncate/ftruncate syscalls if auditctl is in use, which may be required for exhaustive coverage of unsuccessful file modification attempts.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed plan omits the required leading hyphen on the '-a' rule keyword and does not account for whether the system uses auditctl rather than augenrules, potentially rendering the added rules ineffective. Additionally, it does not include truncate/ftruncate syscalls if auditctl is in use, which may be required for exhaustive coverage of unsuccessful file modification attempts.
concerns: Missing leading hyphen on '-a' rule keyword in all lines; rules will not load as written.
Does not detect and handle whether the audit daemon uses augenrules or auditctl; if auditctl is in use, the file in /etc/audit/rules.d will be ignored.
Plan does not include truncate/ftruncate syscalls for auditctl as recommended.
No validation of actual system architecture before writing rules (though harmless, unnecessary lines may be added).
suggested_improvements:
Include the hyphen: '-a always,exit ...' instead of 'a always,exit ...'.
Detect auditctl vs augenrules; if auditctl, write to /etc/audit/audit.rules or use auparse to add rules.
Add 'truncate' and 'ftruncate' syscalls in auditctl rule as recommended.
Optionally check system architecture with $(getconf LONG_BIT) and limit rules to relevant arch(s).
